extraction.one_line_summary: O'CONNOR won from 40+ rider breakaway on Tignes climb, gaining 5 minutes while UAE let 8:13 GC cushion evaporate before Pogacar secured yellow with late attack
tactical_patterns: UAE delayed chase until O'Connor threatened yellow at 8:30 gap, showing precise threshold management rather than blanket controlling.
Quintana attacked 65km out to shed breakaway companions and harvest KOM points, weaponizing long-range aggression for jersey competition.
Ineos paced when O'Connor no longer threatened yellow but Pogacar remained isolated, revealing GC protection hierarchy over team leader support.
Pogacar counter-attacked after being reeled back once, confirming second-wave attacks succeed when rivals exhaust chasing first move.

rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: held 5-minute advantage solo over final 17km after shedding Higuita, suggesting undertapped GC engine at 8:13 pre-stage deficit
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: suffered 'very worrying moment' before final climb while protecting yellow, exposing UAE depth vulnerability on successive mountain stages
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: first dropped from leading trio despite initiating 65km breakaway selection, confirming KOM tactics incompatible with stage-winning form
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: lost 30+ seconds to Pogacar while struggling to hold contact, concerning weakness on sub-maximal tempo before race-defining Alpine stages
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: finished behind Pogacar and dropped to 5th GC, suggesting Ineos pacing for others undermined personal performance
